findings.meaning: Two or more FASTA records share the same identifier.
findings.why_it_matters: Duplicate IDs can break indexing, annotation, mapping, and workflow joins.

suggested_actions.reason: Downstream tools often assume FASTA identifiers are unique.

findings.meaning: One or more sequences contain symbols outside the nucleotide/IUPAC alphabet accepted by the assembly profile.
findings.why_it_matters: Invalid characters can make parsers, aligners, or annotation tools fail or misinterpret records.

suggested_actions.reason: Invalid sequence symbols should be corrected or intentionally recoded before downstream analysis.

findings.meaning: Global or per-sequence N content exceeds the assembly profile threshold.
findings.why_it_matters: High N content can reduce mapping confidence and fragment annotation or polishing steps.

suggested_actions.reason: High ambiguity may indicate unresolved assembly regions or masking problems.
Gap closing or polishing may reduce ambiguity before annotation or mapping.

recommended_next_tools.role: assembly-level evaluation after FASTA validity issues are handled
biological completeness after structural preflight checks

findings.meaning: One or more contigs are shorter than the assembly profile minimum length.
findings.why_it_matters: Very short contigs often add noise to assembly statistics and downstream annotation.

suggested_actions.reason: Short records may be noise, but should be reviewed before automatic removal.

cannot_conclude: whether a tiny contig is biologically real

findings.meaning: The longest contiguous N gap run exceeds the assembly profile limit.
findings.why_it_matters: Long gap runs can indicate unresolved assembly regions and may disrupt mapping or annotation.

suggested_actions.reason: Long gaps may require gap closing, masking review, or scaffold-level inspection.

findings.meaning: Two or more FASTA records contain identical sequence strings.
findings.why_it_matters: Duplicate sequences can inflate assembly metrics and confuse record-level comparisons.

suggested_actions.reason: Repeated sequence records may be expected in some contexts but should be explicit.

findings.meaning: One or more records have GC composition far from the assembly background.
findings.why_it_matters: GC outliers can reflect contamination, cobionts, plasmids, artifacts, or genuine biological variation and need context before interpretation.

suggested_actions.reason: Records with GC composition far from the assembly background should be inspected with coverage and taxonomy context.
K-mer or taxonomy comparison can help distinguish artifacts from plausible biological variation.

recommended_next_tools.role: inspect composition, coverage, and taxonomy context for affected records
compare k-mer similarity when the pattern is strong

findings.meaning: One or more records have lengths outside the assembly length distribution.
findings.why_it_matters: Length outliers can be expected in assemblies, but extreme records deserve inspection before downstream filtering or annotation.

suggested_actions.reason: Extreme record lengths should be reviewed before automatic filtering or annotation.

recommended_next_tools.role: inspect sequence lengths and affected records

cannot_conclude: whether an extreme length is biologically expected

findings.meaning: One or more records combine a composition anomaly with another independent FASTA-level signal.
findings.why_it_matters: Multiple independent FASTA-level signals make a record more likely to need manual review before expensive downstream QC.

suggested_actions.reason: Records with multiple FASTA-level anomaly signals should be prioritized for composition and coverage review.

recommended_next_tools.role: prioritize composition and coverage review for records with multiple signals

findings.meaning: The input file could not be parsed as structurally valid FASTA.
findings.why_it_matters: Structurally invalid FASTA cannot be parsed reliably by downstream tools.

suggested_actions.reason: Every record needs a valid header and sequence data before downstream tools can consume it safely.

recommended_next_tools.tool: text editor or parser-aware cleanup script
recommended_next_tools.role: repair malformed FASTA before rerunning FastaGuard
